title: Simulate survival-count data from the 4PL thermal-load-sensitivity model — simulate_tls
text: beta-binomial family, phi sum Beta shape parameters: counts drawn prob <- rbeta(= p * phi, b = (1 - p) * phi) followed rbinom(n, prob). Beta mean p variance p (1 - p) / (phi + 1), larger phi means less overdispersion binomial recovered phi -> Inf. matches engine's parameterisation beta_binomial_tls().

text: data Raw data frame tibble. temp Column name assay temperature (°C). duration Column name exposure duration. unit whatever source data; record via duration_unit. n_total Column name total individuals per replicate. Required count responses; leave NULL (default) continuous proportion response. n_surv Column name survivor counts. n_dead Column name death counts. Converted n_surv via n_surv = n_total - n_dead. survival Column name survival proportions [0, 1]. Converted integer counts via n_total. mortality Column name mortality proportions [0, 1]. Converted n_surv = round((1 - mortality) * n_total). proportion Column name continuous proportion response [0, 1] denominator (modelled Beta likelihood). Mutually exclusive count arguments . proportion_eps Half-open clamp applied proportion values sit strictly inside (0, 1) (Beta density undefined exactly 0 1). Default 0.001. random_effects Optional character vector grouping variables random effects, e.g. c("Date", "Tank"). columns converted factors stored metadata fitter read. duration_unit Label unit duration, stored metadata. Default "hours". temp_mean Value subtract temp form temp_c. NULL (default) uses mean(temp). Supply fixed value align multiple datasets common centre.

text: tls_bf() captures per-sub-parameter formulas define freqTLS model returns , unevaluated, tls_formula object. formula complement column interface fit_tls(): instead passing bare column names, write one response formula plus formula per model sub-parameter.

text: ... response--axes formula first (unnamed, left-hand side), sub-parameter formulas keyed left-hand side. See grammar .

title: Build a freqTLS formula object (brms/drmTMB-style) — tls_bf
text: tls_formula object: list captured response formula, named sub-parameter formulas, calling environment.

title: Build a freqTLS formula object (brms/drmTMB-style) — tls_bf
text: first argument must unnamed response--axes formula. left-hand side names survival counts, either brms idiom successes | trials(total) glm idiom cbind(successes, failures). right-hand side names two thermal-load-sensitivity axes tagged markers time(<duration>) temp(<temperature>) (order matter). remaining arguments sub-parameter formulas keyed left-hand side, one low, , log_k, CTmax, log_z. sub-parameter omit defaults ~ 1. experimental 0.1.0, low, , log_k may carry design independently — grouping factor (low ~ group), continuous covariate (log_k ~ body_size), intercept — longer required share one factor match CTmax / log_z grouping. CTmax log_z accept fixed-effect formula (e.g. CTmax ~ group). single random intercept, <param> ~ <fixed> + (1 | group), accepted CTmax, log_z, low, log_k (one grouping factor , intercept ) – upper asymptote , compiled objective random-intercept term. Putting grouping factor two fits independent variances (correlation term) warns.

title: Build a freqTLS formula object (brms/drmTMB-style) — tls_bf
text: shape parser (variadic capture via substitute(), per-entry formula walk, random-bar detection) adapted drmTMB's drm_formula() / parse_drm_formula_entry() (GPL-3); see inst/COPYRIGHTS. freqTLS writes grammar (time() / temp() axis markers, five fixed sub-parameter handles, v0.1 restrictions).

title: Build a freqTLS formula object (brms/drmTMB-style) — tls_bf
code: tls_bf(   survived | trials(total) ~ time(duration) + temp(temp),   CTmax ~ life_stage,   log_z ~ life_stage ) #> <tls_formula> #> survived | trials(total) ~ time(duration) + temp(temp) #> CTmax ~ life_stage #> log_z ~ life_stage # cbind() response idiom, ungrouped: tls_bf(cbind(survived, died) ~ time(duration) + temp(temp)) #> <tls_formula> #> cbind(survived, died) ~ time(duration) + temp(temp)

what: Response families for thermal-load-sensitivity models — tls_family
title: Response families for thermal-load-sensitivity models — tls_family
text: binomial_tls() beta_binomial_tls() describe count response distribution fit_tls(), beta_tls() continuous-proportion response (0, 1) (e.g. PSII operating efficiency relative chlorophyll fluorescence). three model survival four-parameter logistic function log10 duration; beta-binomial beta families add dispersion parameter phi.

title: Response families for thermal-load-sensitivity models — tls_family
code: binomial_tls()  beta_binomial_tls()  beta_tls()

title: Response families for thermal-load-sensitivity models — tls_family
text: tls_family object: list family, family_code (0 binomial, 1 beta-binomial, 2 beta), links natural-scale parameters.

title: Response families for thermal-load-sensitivity models — tls_family
text: phi convention beta-binomial family sum Beta shape parameters: fitted survival probability p, counts Beta-Binomial shapes = p * phi b = (1 - p) * phi. Larger phi means less overdispersion (binomial recovered phi grows). matches simulation convention simulate_tls() differs precision/size parameterisations used packages. beta family uses shapes continuous proportion, y ~ Beta(p * phi, (1 - p) * phi), phi carries identical meaning larger phi means tighter response around fitted curve.

what: Uncertainty for the classical two-stage TDT quantities — ts_ci
title: Uncertainty for the classical two-stage TDT quantities — ts_ci
text: Two propagation methods Stage-2 fit: "delta" — delta-method standard errors z CTmax, Normal t quantiles (t-quantile small-sample correction Stage-2 residual degrees freedom). method bias simulation reports. "mvn" — slope-CI inversion z (defined slope CI wholly negative) plus MVN simulation Stage-2 coefficients CTmax T_crit, predict.lm confidence band LT-vs-T line temp_grid. method case studies report.

title: Uncertainty for the classical two-stage TDT quantities — ts_ci
code: ts_ci(   stage2,   method = c("delta", "mvn"),   level = 0.95,   t_ref = 60,   time_multiplier = 1,   TC_rate_range = c(0.1, 1),   temp_grid = NULL,   n_sim = 1000,   seed = 123 )

title: Uncertainty for the classical two-stage TDT quantities — ts_ci
text: stage2 Output ts_stage2(). method "delta" "mvn". level Confidence level. Default 0.95. t_ref, time_multiplier, TC_rate_range ts_stage2(). temp_grid Temperatures line band ("mvn" ). n_sim MVN draws ("mvn" ). Default 1000. seed RNG seed ("mvn" ). Default 123.

title: Uncertainty for the classical two-stage TDT quantities — ts_ci
text: "delta", list z CTmax_1hr, list(point, lower, upper, lower_t, upper_t, se), plus df_resid. "mvn", list summary_ci (z/CTmax/T_crit bounds) curve_ci (per-temp_grid line band).

title: Uncertainty for the classical two-stage TDT quantities — ts_ci
code: d <- data.frame(   temp = rep(c(30, 32, 34, 36, 38), each = 12),   dur  = rep(c(1, 5, 15, 45, 135, 405), times = 10),   surv = rbinom(60, 20, 0.4), tot = 20) s2 <- ts_stage2(ts_stage1(d, "temp", "dur", "surv", "tot")) ts_ci(s2, method = "delta")$z #> $point #> [1] NA #>  #> $lower #> [1] NA #>  #> $upper #> [1] NA #>  #> $lower_t #> [1] NA #>  #> $upper_t #> [1] NA #>  #> $se #> [1] NA #>

what: Stage 1 of the classical two-stage TDT pipeline — ts_stage1
title: Stage 1 of the classical two-stage TDT pipeline — ts_stage1
text: Fits separate logistic dose-response curve assay temperature reads log10(LT50) (duration 50% survival). binomial family uses stats::glm; beta-binomial family uses glmmTMB::glmmTMB betabinomial family (overdispersion Stage 1).

title: Stage 1 of the classical two-stage TDT pipeline — ts_stage1
code: ts_stage1(   data,   temp = "temp",   duration = "duration",   n_surv = "n_surv",   n_total = "n_total",   family = c("binomial", "betabinomial") )

title: Stage 1 of the classical two-stage TDT pipeline — ts_stage1
text: data Data frame one row per (temperature, duration) replicate. temp, duration, n_surv, n_total Column names (strings) assay temperature (°C), exposure duration, survivors, trials. family "binomial" "betabinomial".

title: Stage 1 of the classical two-stage TDT pipeline — ts_stage1
text: tibble one row per temperature: temp, log10_lt50, se_log10_lt50, slope, phi (beta-binomial precision, else NA), finite_ok, bracket_ok, stage1_ok.

title: Stage 1 of the classical two-stage TDT pipeline — ts_stage1
text: Two validity flags returned callers can choose success rule: finite_ok (finite coefficients, negative non-trivial slope) bracket_ok (fitted LT50 lies within observed duration range, padded 0.5 log10 scale). stage1_ok conjunction.

what: Stage 2 of the classical two-stage TDT pipeline — ts_stage2
title: Stage 2 of the classical two-stage TDT pipeline — ts_stage2
text: Regresses Stage-1 log10(LT50) assay temperature ordinary least squares derives classical quantities. z = -1/slope; CTmax(t_ref) = (log10(t_ref) - intercept) / slope; T_crit follows rate-multiplier definition, CTmax + z * mean(log10(TC_rate_range/100)).

title: Stage 2 of the classical two-stage TDT pipeline — ts_stage2
code: ts_stage2(   stage1,   t_ref = 60,   time_multiplier = 1,   TC_rate_range = c(0.1, 1),   rows = c("stage1_ok", "finite_ok") )

title: Stage 2 of the classical two-stage TDT pipeline — ts_stage2
text: stage1 Output ts_stage1(). t_ref Reference exposure duration CTmax (minutes). Default 60. time_multiplier Multiplier Stage-1 duration unit minutes (e.g. 60 durations hours). Default 1. TC_rate_range Length-2 HI-rate range (% per hour) T_crit. rows Stage-1 rows keep: "stage1_ok" (bracketing validation, case-study default) "finite_ok" (finite/negative slope , simulation's looser rule).

title: Stage 2 of the classical two-stage TDT pipeline — ts_stage2
text: list(fit, summary); fit NULL fewer 3 valid Stage-1 estimates remain. summary intercept, slope_T, z, CTmax_1hr, T_crit, r_squared, n_stage1, n_excluded.

title: Stage 2 of the classical two-stage TDT pipeline — ts_stage2
code: d <- data.frame(   temp = rep(c(30, 32, 34, 36, 38), each = 12),   dur  = rep(c(1, 5, 15, 45, 135, 405), times = 10),   surv = rbinom(60, 20, 0.4), tot = 20) s1 <- ts_stage1(d, "temp", "dur", "surv", "tot") ts_stage2(s1)$summary #> # A tibble: 1 × 8 #>   intercept slope_T     z CTmax_1hr T_crit r_squared n_stage1 n_excluded #>       <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl>  <dbl>     <dbl>    <int>      <int> #> 1        NA      NA    NA        NA     NA        NA        0          5

title: freqTLS 0.1.0 (development)
text: freqTLS frequentist counterpart Bayesian bayesTLS package: fits four-parameter logistic thermal-load-sensitivity (thermal death-time) model maximum likelihood via TMB, parameterised directly CTmax thermal sensitivity (z). bayesTLS analysis run freqTLS changing package data functions come ; uncertainty reported frequentist trio — Wald (delta), profile-likelihood, bootstrap — instead posterior. Forked profileTLS (commit 6f963a9, v0.3.3), supersedes.

title: freqTLS 0.1.0 (development)
text: standardize_data() — shared raw-data entry point count continuous-proportion responses (adopted bayesTLS). fit_4pl() + make_4pl_formula() — direct CTmax/z formula interface (ctmax/z//low/k/, plus threshold, t_ref, bounds, family), fitted maximum likelihood TMB engine; returns freq_tls workflow object. tls() / tls_z() / tls_ctmax() / tls_tcrit() — z, CTmax, T_crit confidence intervals relative midpoint, absolute (LT50) threshold, LTx. extract_tdt() get_z_* / get_ctmax_* / get_tcrit_* accessors — nested z / CTmax / T_crit structure, parametric-bootstrap replicates frequentist analogue posterior draws. predict_survival_curves() — fitted survival surface bootstrap bands. diagnose_tdt_fit() tdt_parameter_table() — convergence diagnostics (optimiser/Hessian/gradient) 4PL parameter table. two_stage (ts_stage1() / ts_stage2() / ts_ci() / ts_curve()) — classical two-stage comparator, reporting normal small-sample t intervals. plots (plot_confidence_eye(), plot_survival_curves(), plot_tdt_curve(), plot_heat_injury()) extractors accept freq_tls workflow object. Seven shared case-study datasets, including aphid_tdt (Li et al. 2023) zebrafish_o2 (Saruhashi et al. 2026).

title: freqTLS 0.1.0 (development)
text: Small-sample Bates–Watts profile-t / Wald-t calibration: confidence intervals reference t distribution residual df = n − p, restoring nominal coverage small n reducing asymptotic interval n grows. evidence coverage + width simulation (data-raw/calibration-study.R): df ≈ 10 asymptotic 95% interval covers ~0.93 t-correction restores ~0.96. three-way benchmark (data-raw/benchmark-vs-bayes.R): freqTLS reproduces bayesTLS’s CTmax ~0.07 °C brown-shrimp data, beside classical two-stage estimator.

title: freqTLS 0.1.0 (development)
text: confint(), summary(), ranef(), coef()/logLik()/vcov()/nobs(), heat-injury functions (predict_heat_injury() / plot_heat_injury() / heat_injury_envelope()), check_tls() accept freq_tls workflow object — whole post-fit surface works fit_4pl() result. fit_4pl(= "g") now labels groups bare factor levels (CTmax:young_embryos), identical column interface, end end.
